name: astroquery (Astronomy Database Query Library)

auth.notes: Some services require API key (ESA, NASA); many services are public

performance.notes: Network-bound; large image/catalog downloads can take minutes

agent_readiness.notes: Query 100+ astronomical databases. from astroquery.simbad import Simbad. result = Simbad.query_object('M31'). from astroquery.vizier import Vizier. v = Vizier(catalog='J/ApJS/220/6'). tables = v.get_catalogs('J/ApJS/220/6'). from astroquery.mast import Observations. obs = Observations.query_object('Kepler-10', radius='0.1 deg'). from astroquery.gaia import Gaia. job = Gaia.launch_job('SELECT * FROM gaiadr3.gaia_source LIMIT 100'). from astroquery.ned import Ned. Simbad, VizieR, MAST, Gaia, NED, ESA, NASA HEASARC, SDSS, 2MASS.

gotchas: Each service has different API — no unified interface
Rate limiting enforced by servers — add delays for bulk queries
Service availability depends on external servers
best_when: Querying SIMBAD, VizieR, Gaia, MAST, NED astronomical databases
Downloading catalog data for stars, galaxies, sources
Cross-matching with Gaia DR3 or 2MASS catalogs
Getting Kepler/TESS observations metadata from MAST
avoid_when: Production pipelines without rate limit handling
When direct API access to specific service is more efficient
summary: astroquery queries 100+ astronomical databases from Python. Simbad.query_object('M31'). Vizier.get_catalogs(). Observations.query_object() from MAST. Gaia.launch_job(ADQL). NED cross-identification. BSD-3 by Astropy. Standard library for astronomical data access. Each service has different API. Rate-limit-aware querying needed for bulk use. Covers SIMBAD, VizieR, Gaia, MAST, NED, SDSS, 2MASS.
